channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [General] Get More Leads: Your Secret to More Reviews #shorts
item.description: Struggling to get reviews? If you're not running ads and rely on referrals, you might be missing out. Servicing clients and doing great jobs leads to more word-of-mouth and repeat business, naturally increasing your customer base and review count. #BusinessGrowth #CustomerReviews #MarketingTips #Lea

item.title: [General] Boost Your Google Rankings with Review Velocity! #shorts
item.description: Focus on review velocity: how many new 5-star reviews you get monthly. Google favors businesses with high review counts, especially from local guides who add photos. This engagement drives higher rankings. #ReviewVelocity #GoogleMyBusiness #LocalSEO #BusinessGrowth

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Partners With AWS on Graviton Chips to Power Agentic AI
[General] KitKat Canada’s “KatKit” Mix-Up : How the Courage Campaign Launched Mix-Ups With a Simple Brand Twist
item.description: If you’ve seen a KitKat logo turned into “KatKit” and wondered whether it was a printing error, a one-off stunt, or part of something bigger, the short answer is simple: it was a deliberate campaign by KitKat Canada to introduce a new product called Mix-Ups. The execution was intentionally small

item.title: [General] Paramount+ App Redesign 2026: What the New Short-Form Video Feed Means for Streaming, Discovery, and Engagement
item.description: For years, streaming apps were built around a simple assumption: people opened them when they were ready to sit down and watch something for a meaningful stretch of time. The interface reflected that behavior. Large title art, rows of shows, personalized carousels, and a home screen designed to p

item.title: [General] Mattel’s 400-Drone He-Man Billboards at Coachella: What Happened, Why They Worked, and What Brands Can Learn
item.description: When people talk about outdoor advertising, they usually mean roadside placements, transit media, stadium signage, or large-format digital screens. Mattel’s Masters of the Universe activation in the California desert pushed that definition further. Instead of buying attention only on the side of

item.title: [General] Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra and The Devil Wears Prada 2: What the Collaboration Means for Fashion, Film, AI Features, and Premium Smartphone Marketing
item.description: Samsung has found a clean way to place the Galaxy S26 Ultra inside a cultural conversation that already knows how to command attention. Instead of treating the phone launch as a standalone hardware story, the company tied it to The Devil Wears Prada 2, a sequel that arrives with built-in recognit

item.title: [General] Universal Pictures’ Michael Architectural DOOH Campaign – How Milan and Berlin Turned Landmark Buildings Into Movie Media
item.description: When Universal Pictures International launched the outdoor campaign for Michael, it did not use digital billboards in the usual sense. Instead, it used architecture itself as the medium. Two prominent European locations, Rinascente in Milan and Uber Platz in Berlin, were transformed into large-sc

item.title: [General] L’Oréal Paris and Ulta Beauty’s “Mother & ___” Campaign With Eva Longoria: What the 2026 Mother’s Day Beauty Retail Push Means
item.description: Mother’s Day campaigns often follow a familiar pattern. Brands focus on gift giving, sentiment, and broad emotional language that treats motherhood as a single, all-defining identity. L’Oréal Paris and Ulta Beauty took a different approach in 2026. Their “Mother & ___” campaign, led by Eva Lo

item.title: [General] Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen Comes to New York: Menu, Locations, Delivery Strategy, and What NYC Diners Can Expect
item.description: When Gwyneth Paltrow’s Goop Kitchen announced its move into New York, the news landed at the intersection of several trends that have been building for years: the normalization of premium takeout, the rise of ingredient-conscious dining, the steady expansion of delivery-first restaurant models, a

item.title: [General] Espolòn Tequila and Ken Jeong’s Short King Week Campaign
item.description: When a spirits brand tries to join a cultural moment, the risk is usually obvious: the campaign can feel borrowed, rushed, or detached from the product itself. That is what makes Espolòn Tequila’s Short King Week campaign with Ken Jeong more interesting than the average celebrity-backed drinks pr

item.title: [General] OpenAI Adds CPC Ads to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI has begun testing cost-per-click advertising in ChatGPT, and that change matters far beyond media buying mechanics. It signals that ChatGPT is no longer being treated only as a product people use for answers, research, and productivity. It is also being developed into an advertising enviro

item.title: [General] Google Chrome’s New Tab Page Is Becoming an AI Search Hub: Deep Search, Create Images, Tab Context, and What Changes for Users
item.description: For years, the Chrome new tab page was one of the simplest surfaces in consumer software. Open a tab, see a logo, type a query, move on. That simplicity is starting to change. What appeared to be a straightforward search box is increasingly being used to introduce more advanced Google search beha

item.title: [Meta Ads] A few weeks ago someone called me out about my creative roadmaps living in G sheets...
item.description:
I was offended.
However... she was RIGHT.
My friends at Replit saw this going down and offered their new Agent 4 to help.
But even though I had never vibe coded anything IN MY LIFE...
Within minutes, I had a working agency dashboard that tracks tests across all my clients.
ngl, it made me feel

Instead of

item.title: [General] Starbucks Devil Wears Prada 2 Secret Menu: Every Drink, How to Order, Availability, and What the 2026 Partnership Means
item.description: Starbucks has turned a familiar movie detail into a full retail campaign. item.title: [General] How B2B Brands Get on AI Shortlists: A Practical Guide to AI Visibility, Google AI Overviews, and LLM Citations

That model still matters, but it is no longer the full picture. Buyers now ask AI systems to summarize categories, compare vendors, explain trade-offs, recommend

item.title: [General] Top AI Tools for Paid Search Campaigns: 11 Platforms That Improve PPC Research, Optimization, and Conversion Rates

What has changed is the amount of data, the number of decisions, and the pace at which those decisions now need to happen. Teams are expected to manage search queries, bids, budgets, creative testing, landing pages, reporting,

item.title: [General] B2B Marketing Beyond Lead Generation: 7 Tactics to Increase Qualified Pipeline, Buying-Group Engagement, and Revenue
item.description: For years, B2B marketing teams were trained to treat lead volume as the clearest proof of performance. More downloads, more form fills, more names in the CRM, more activity for sales. On paper, that looked efficient. In practice, it often produced the wrong outcome: a large list of contacts that

item.title: [General] Home Depot Retail Media Strategy: What Orange Apron Media’s Reddit, Pinterest, and Measurement Expansion Means for Brands
item.description: Retail media is no longer a side business for a handful of large marketplaces. It has become one of the clearest ways for retailers to turn shopper intent, first-party data, and owned customer relationships into advertising value. In that context, Home Depot’s latest moves matter well beyond the

item.title: [General] How to Evaluate AI Marketing Tools: 5 Questions to Ask Before You Buy Another Platform

item.title: [General] YouTube and SiriusXM Audio Ad Partnership – What It Means for Podcast Advertising, Media Buying, and Audio Strategy
item.description: For years, YouTube sat in an odd position in the media business. It was clearly one of the largest platforms for music, talk, creator-led content, and podcasts, but it was still often discussed as a video company first. That framing is now harder to sustain. The new partnership that makes SiriusX

item.title: [General] Barbie Marketing Strategy in 2026: How Mattel Uses Fandom, Partnerships, and Experiential Marketing to Grow the Brand

item.title: [General] Polymarket and Kalshi in TV Programming: How Prediction Markets Are Reshaping News, Awards Shows, and Audience Engagement

What has changed in the last two years is the toolset. Polls, social chat

item.title: [General] Pinterest’s “Get Off Social Media” Ad Campaign : Why the Platform Wants Users to Go Offline
item.description: Pinterest has spent years trying to separate itself from the rest of the social platform pack, but its latest brand campaign makes that distinction clearer than ever. Instead of encouraging more scrolling, more posting, and more time spent in-app, Pinterest is making a very different case: the be

item.title: [General] LinkedIn Expands AI-Powered Conversational Search to All Users: What Changed and How to Show Up in Results
item.description: LinkedIn search is changing in a way that matters far beyond one product update. The platform has expanded its AI-powered conversational search experience from a limited rollout to broader member access, which means users can increasingly search the way they naturally think and speak instead of r

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Simplifies Ad Performance Elements: What the New Pixel AI and One-Click Conversions API Mean for Advertisers
item.description: Meta has introduced two important updates to the infrastructure behind ad performance: a new AI-assisted enhancement for Meta Pixel and a simpler, one-click path to set up Conversions API for web. On the surface, that sounds like a technical product update. In practice, it is much more significan

item.title: [General] How X Cashtags Work: New Format, Live Price Charts, and What It Means for Market Analysis
item.description: For years, X has been one of the fastest places on the internet for price-sensitive information. Traders watch it for headlines, crypto communities use it to coordinate attention, and public companies often see market narratives take shape there before they fully spread elsewhere. That has made t

item.title: [General] Google Crawler IP Ranges in 2026: New File Location, Verification Steps, Firewall Rules, and What Site Owners Need to Update
item.description: If your website relies on firewall rules, CDN allowlists, bot management, or server-side verification to identify legitimate Google crawlers, Google’s March 2026 update matters immediately. The change itself is simple: the published JSON files for Google crawler IP ranges have moved from the olde

item.title: [General] Top Brand Monitoring Tools in 2026: Free and Paid Platforms Compared by Features, Pricing, and Best Use Case
item.description: Brand monitoring has changed. A few years ago, most teams could get by with a simple mention alert and a social media dashboard. In 2026, that is no longer enough. A brand can be discussed on news sites, review platforms, Reddit threads, YouTube comments, LinkedIn posts, podcasts, forums, newslet

item.title: [Meta Ads] 50,000 new customers per month without media buyer or a creative strategist. HOW?!
item.description:
The apparel brand Pact acquires 50,000 new customers per month.
They don’t have a media buyer or a creative strategist on staff.
So… how the heck are they doing this?
In the final episode of Ad Spend, I sit down with Kaitlin Parker, Pact’s Chief Digital Officer, to discuss how they approach growt

item.title: [General] AI Video Generator: Beat Competition & Rank High #shorts
item.description: Understanding your competition is key for any product. For AI video generators, a crowded market demands strategic promotion and SEO to get on trusted lists like Zapier, Reddit, and Quora. #AIvideo #Competition #MarketingStrategy #SEOtips #VideoGeneration

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Budget Pacing Explained
item.description: Most PPC managers treat budget changes like a dial. Turn it up, get more sales. Turn it down, save money. The reality inside Google Ads is messier, slower, and a lot more expensive when you get it wrong. Pacing is not a setting. It is a behavior the system learns, and every edit teaches it [̷

item.title: [General] AI vs Human Ad Copy: Google vs Meta #shorts
item.description: AI ad copy can outperform humans, but human touch is crucial. Don't let your ads sound robotic. Especially on Meta, where copywriting truly matters. Explore the balance. #AdCopy #AICopywriting #MarketingTips #DigitalMarketing

item.title: [General] 魔法の施策は存在しない。泥臭いPDCAでCVを2倍にした広告運用の振り返り
item.description:
「成果改善の一発逆転ホームラン施策はないだろうか」「媒体仕様をハックして、競合を出し抜けないか」「今すぐCPAを下げたいんだけど、配信量を下げるしかないか…」 場合によっては、1つの設定を変えるだけで…

The post 魔法の施策は存在しない。泥臭いPDCAでCVを2倍にした広告運用の振り返り first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.

item.title: [Meta Ads] No-Cost, One-Click Conversions API Solution

item.description: Microsoft Advertising has expanded its Google import capabilities again, and this time the update matters for advertisers that care about growth quality, not just campaign portability. item.title: [General] Best AI Sales Outreach Tools in 2026: 20 Platforms Compared for Email, LinkedIn, Calling, and AI SDR Workflows
item.description: Sales outreach software has changed fast, but the core job has not. Teams still need to identify the right accounts, reach the right contacts, personalize at scale, follow up consistently, and turn activity into meetings and pipeline. What has changed is the amount of manual work that can now be

item.title: [General] Inside the U.S. Navy Healthcare Recruiting Campaign: Why VML and WPP Focused on Physicians, Nurses, and Dentists in 4 Major Markets
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s new healthcare recruiting campaign is notable not because it is loud, broad, or designed for mass appeal, but because it does the opposite. Instead of speaking to everyone, it speaks to a narrow professional audience: early-career physicians, nurses, and dentists, especially those
